questions.text: Most tribes were located in:
options: Remote areas like forests, hills, and deserts
questions.explanation: Tribal societies lived in difficult-to-reach areas to preserve their independence and culture.

questions.text: Tribal societies did not follow the social rules and rituals prescribed by the:
questions.explanation: Tribal societies were egalitarian and did not follow the varna-based caste system.

questions.text: The term used for people who move from one place to another is:
questions.explanation: Nomadic pastoralists moved long distances with their animals.

questions.text: Which powerful tribe controlled the Punjab during the 13th and 14th centuries?
questions.explanation: The Khokhar tribe was very influential in the Punjab region.

questions.text: Who was the Gakkhar chief made a noble (mansabdar) by Emperor Akbar?
questions.explanation: The Gakkhars became more important after the Khokhars, and their chief was recognized by Akbar.

questions.text: The Balochis were a large and powerful tribe in the:
questions.explanation: They were divided into many smaller clans led by different chiefs.

questions.text: In the western Himalaya lived the shepherd tribe of:
questions.explanation: The Gaddis were a pastoral tribe in the Himalayan region.

questions.text: Tribes in the North-eastern part of the subcontinent were dominated by:
questions.explanation: The Nagas, Ahoms, and many others completely dominated this region.

questions.text: Which tribe was found across vast areas of western and central India?
questions.explanation: By the late 16th century, many Bhils had become settled agriculturists and some even zamindars.

questions.text: The Gonds lived in a vast forested region called:
questions.explanation: Gondwana means 'country inhabited by Gonds'.

questions.text: Gonds practiced ________ cultivation.
questions.explanation: Shifting cultivation involves clearing a patch of forest, burning it, and sowing crops in the ashes.

questions.text: A 'clan' is a group of families or households claiming descent from a:
questions.explanation: Tribal organization is often based on kinship or clan loyalties.

questions.text: The Gond kingdom was divided into administrative units called:
questions.explanation: Each garh was controlled by a particular Gond clan.

questions.text: A 'Garh' was further divided into units of 84 villages called:
questions.explanation: Chaurasi literally means 84.

questions.text: The Chaurasi was further subdivided into 'Barhots' which had ________ villages each.
questions.explanation: The administration was becoming centralized as large Gond kingdoms emerged.

questions.text: Who was the Rani of Garha Katanga who fought against the Mughal forces?
questions.explanation: Rani Durgawati ruled on behalf of her young son and died fighting the Mughals in 1565.

questions.text: Garha Katanga was a rich state that earned wealth by trapping and exporting:
questions.explanation: The export of wild elephants was a major source of revenue for the Gond kingdom.

questions.text: The Ahoms migrated to the Brahmaputra valley from present-day:
questions.explanation: They migrated in the 13th century and created a new state by suppressing older landlords.

questions.text: The Ahoms suppressed the older political system of the 'bhuiyans', which means:
questions.explanation: By defeating the bhuiyans, the Ahoms established their own authority.

questions.text: The Ahom state depended upon forced labour, and those forced to work were called:
questions.explanation: A census of the population was taken, and each village had to send a number of paiks by rotation.

questions.text: The Ahom society was divided into clans or:
questions.explanation: A khel often controlled several villages.

questions.text: Historical works written by the Ahoms were called:
questions.explanation: Buranjis were written first in the Ahom language and then in Assamese.

questions.text: Who were the most important trader-nomads?
questions.explanation: Their caravan was called a 'tanda'. Sultan Alauddin Khalji used them to transport grain to city markets.

questions.text: Nomadic pastoralists lived on milk and other pastoral products, and exchanged these for:
questions.explanation: They traded items they obtained from their animals with settled agriculturists.

questions.text: What is 'shifting cultivation'?
options: Moving from one village to another
Trees and bushes are cut and burnt; crops are sown in the ashes
questions.explanation: When this land loses its fertility, another patch of land is cleared.

questions.text: The Mughals under ________ attacked and defeated Garha Katanga in 1565.
questions.explanation: The Mughal forces led by Asaf Khan defeated the Gonds despite their strong resistance.

questions.text: As the Gond kingdoms weakened, they were later attacked by the:
questions.explanation: The Gond kingdoms struggled against the rising power of the Bundelas and Marathas.

questions.text: The Ahoms built a large state using ________ in the 1660s.
questions.explanation: The Ahoms were technologically advanced in warfare.

questions.text: The Mughal army under ________ captured the Ahom capital Garhgaon in 1662.
questions.explanation: Despite the Ahom resistance, the Mughals managed to capture their capital.

questions.text: In the Ahom state, almost all adult males served in the ________ during war.
questions.explanation: The Ahom state had a system where citizens contributed military service.

questions.text: Ahoms introduced new methods of ________ cultivation.
questions.explanation: Their agricultural innovations helped support a growing population.

questions.text: Under whose reign did Hinduism become the predominant religion of the Ahoms?
questions.explanation: During Sib Singh's reign (1714-1744), Hinduism became very important, but kings did not give up traditional beliefs.

questions.text: The Gond Raja of Garha Katanga, Aman Das, assumed the title of:
questions.explanation: He took the title to gain status and associate himself with Rajput traditions.

questions.text: Many tribes were divided into numerous smaller:
questions.explanation: Clans were the basic unit of social and political organization in tribes.

questions.text: Why are there few written records of tribal people?
options: They did not keep written records; preserved customs orally
The records were destroyed by fire
questions.explanation: Historians use these rich oral traditions to write tribal histories.

questions.text: Chero chiefdoms had emerged in the 12th century in present-day:
questions.explanation: Raja Man Singh, Akbar's general, attacked and defeated them in 1591.

questions.text: The Mundas and Santhals were important tribes in:
questions.explanation: They lived in these regions and also in Jharkhand.

questions.text: Kolis and Berads lived in the highlands of:
questions.explanation: Kolis were also found in many areas of Gujarat.

questions.text: Which community of people were essentially wandering singers and dancers?
questions.explanation: These groups moved from village to village performing for their livelihood.

questions.text: As the economy and needs of society grew, people with new skills were required. This led to:
options: Emergence of new Jatis within Varnas
questions.explanation: Specialized artisans like smiths, carpenters, and masons were recognized as separate jatis.
